response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and distributed systems engineer with a long career, but he critically fails to meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — a gap so large it disqualifies him outright. His primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has some exposure to distributed systems and DevOps tooling, there is no evidence of leading a cloud compute platform or managing infrastructure at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Broad distributed systems exposure, including micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Deep commitment to engineering quality: TDD advocacy, testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts), Selenium courses, and Code Kata sessions demonstrate strong software craftsmanship
Long, continuous career (30+ years) spanning embedded, web, mobile, and AI-agent infrastructure, showing adaptability and breadth
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments + one thesis project) against an 8-year hard minimum requirement — this alone disqualifies the candidate
No evidence of cloud compute platform ownership or leadership; cloud exposure is limited to using AWS CDK and Kubernetes as a consumer, not as a platform builder or architect
Technical leadership is informal and workshop-based (Code Katas, Selenium courses) with no documented record of leading engineering teams or driving large-scale platform strategy
